title: Recognizing that members and affiliates of Tren de Aragua are alien enemies perpetrating an invasion of the United States and affirming that the President is exercising his constitutional authority to repel that invasion.
plain_summary: This bill says that a dangerous gang called Tren de Aragua from other countries is invading the United States. The bill calls these gang members enemies of America. It supports the President using special laws to arrest these gang members and kick them out of the country. The bill treats this gang like an enemy army instead of just regular criminals.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution recognizes that members and people connected to a criminal group called Tren de Aragua are foreign enemies who are invading the United States. It also affirms that the President has the constitutional power to fight back against this invasion.

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: The Constitution gives the President executive power and makes him the Commander in Chief of the military. In seventeen ninety-eight, Congress passed a law called the Alien Enemies Act that allows the President to remove foreign enemies during an invasion. This law says that when there is a declared war or invasion by a foreign nation or government, the President can arrest and remove male non-citizens who are fourteen years old or older from the hostile nation. When this law was written, the word invasion included actions by groups that are not official governments. On January twentieth, two thousand twenty-five, President Trump declared Tren de Aragua a foreign terrorist organization. The order describes how this group carries out violent and terrifying campaigns in the United States and around the world that threaten stability in the Western Hemisphere. A confirmed Tren de Aragua member killed twenty-year-old nursing student Laken Hope Riley. Two illegal immigrants from Venezuela who are believed to have ties to Tren de Aragua were charged with murdering and sexually assaulting twelve-year-old Jocelyn Nungaray on June sixteenth, two thousand twenty-four. Law enforcement has confirmed that Tren de Aragua has taken over apartment buildings at gunpoint in cities including Aurora, Colorado and San Antonio, Texas. In March two thousand twenty-four, more than one hundred suspected Tren de Aragua members were arrested for charging at National Guard troops and state troopers at the El Paso border. In June two thousand twenty-four, a Tren de Aragua member was charged with shooting and injuring two New York City police officers when they tried to arrest him. On July thirtieth, two thousand twenty-four, the Border Patrol warned that Tren de Aragua told its more than one thousand members to shoot at and attack law enforcement. As of March two thousand twenty-five, Tren de Aragua is present in at least nineteen states. On March fifteenth, two thousand twenty-five, President Trump issued a proclamation using the Alien Enemies Act. This proclamation states that Tren de Aragua is carrying out, attempting, and threatening an invasion of the United States. The proclamation also declares that Tren de Aragua is taking hostile actions and conducting irregular warfare against the United States both directly and under orders from the Maduro government in Venezuela. In the previous Congress, more than eighty members sponsored legislation stating that mass illegal immigration caused by open-border policies was an invasion of the United States. Removing foreign enemies who are invading the United States is necessary to keep Americans safe and is a proper use of the President's executive power.

sections.title: What the House Resolves
sections.text: The House of Representatives recognizes that Tren de Aragua is a terrorist organization that is invading the United States both directly and under orders from a foreign government. The House recognizes that members and people connected to Tren de Aragua are foreign enemies. The House recognizes and affirms that the President is using his constitutional and legal authority to fight back against an invasion by foreign enemies by arresting, restraining, securing, and removing members and people connected to Tren de Aragua from the United States of America.
